name: sdkwork-utils
description: Cross-language utility API contract. All language packages must implement these modules and operations with equivalent behavior.

semantics.blank: null, undefined, None, or whitespace-only strings are blank.
semantics.string_length: max_len and pad targets use Unicode code points (characters), not UTF-8 bytes.
semantics.datetime: All instants are UTC. Default parse/format pattern is ISO-8601 with millisecond precision.
semantics.path_separator: Logical path segments use forward slash regardless of OS.
semantics.failure: Parse/decode failures return null/None/optional empty; they do not throw unless language idioms require explicit Result types.
semantics.naming: Contract operations use snake_case. Each language package maps to idiomatic export names documented in specs/naming.aliases.json.
semantics.currency: ISO 4217 codes are 3 uppercase ASCII letters. Minor units use specs/currency.metadata.json exponents. format_currency uses locale separators from i18n rules and symbol placement from currency metadata.
semantics.bloom: SHA-256 Kirsch-Mitzenmacher hashing: h1/h2 from first 8 digest bytes (big-endian u32), positions (h1 + i*h2) mod m. m = max(64, ceil(-n*ln(p)/ln(2)^2)), k = max(1, round(m/n*ln(2))).
semantics.template: Replace {identifier} placeholders when the key exists in the values map. Identifiers match [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*. Unknown placeholders are left unchanged. No expression evaluation.
semantics.bytes: Byte counts use binary (1024) scaling with units B, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB. Negative inputs are treated as 0. Whole-byte values under 1024 render as integer B without decimals.
semantics.base64url: URL-safe base64 uses - and _ without padding on encode; decode accepts optional padding.
semantics.ipv6: IPv6 shape check for full and ::-compressed forms; excludes IPv4 dotted strings.
semantics.e164: E.164 shape is + followed by 2-15 decimal digits with first digit 1-9.
semantics.parse_number_locale: Parse locale-grouped number strings for en-US and de-DE separators matching format_number_locale.

string.is_blank: Returns true when value is null/None/undefined or whitespace-only.
string.truncate: Truncates to max length with optional suffix (default '...'). Uses Unicode code points.
string.capitalize: Uppercases first character, lowercases the rest.
string.mask: Masks middle of string, keeping visible start/end counts. Default mask char '*'.
string.pad_start: Pads string on the left to target length. Default pad char space.
string.pad_end: Pads string on the right to target length. Default pad char space.
string.starts_with: True when value begins with prefix (case-sensitive).
string.ends_with: True when value ends with suffix (case-sensitive).
string.replace_all: Replace all occurrences of search with replacement.
string.split: Split string by delimiter into non-empty trimmed parts when trim_parts is true.
string.repeat: Repeat string count times; count must be >= 0.
string.normalize_whitespace: Trim and collapse internal whitespace runs to a single space.
string.template: Substitute {key} placeholders from a string map; unknown keys remain literal.

datetime.parse: Parse datetime string; default pattern ISO-8601.
datetime.diff_millis: Milliseconds from earlier to later (later - earlier).
datetime.is_before: True when first instant is strictly before second.
datetime.is_after: True when first instant is strictly after second.
datetime.start_of_day_utc: UTC midnight for the given instant's date.
datetime.end_of_day_utc: UTC 23:59:59.999 for the given instant's date.
datetime.to_unix_millis: Convert UTC instant to Unix epoch milliseconds.
datetime.from_unix_millis: Convert Unix epoch milliseconds to UTC instant.
datetime.is_same_instant: True when two UTC instants represent the same millisecond.

number.parse_number: Parse string to number or null/none on failure.
number.is_integer: True when value is an integer.
number.parse_int: Parse string to integer or null/none on failure.
number.percent_format: Format ratio (0-1) as percentage string with fixed decimals.
number.in_range: True when value is within inclusive [min, max].

collection.chunk: Split into fixed-size groups; last chunk may be smaller.
collection.flatten: Flatten one level of nested lists.
collection.compact: Remove null/none entries from a list preserving order.
collection.first: Return first element or null/none when empty.
collection.last: Return last element or null/none when empty.
collection.sort_by: Return a new list sorted ascending by key function.
collection.key_by: Build a map from list items keyed by key function; later items overwrite earlier keys.
collection.filter: Return items matching predicate, preserving order.
collection.find: Return first item matching predicate or null/none.

validation.is_numeric: True when entire string parses as number.
validation.is_ipv4: True when string is dotted IPv4 address.
validation.is_ipv6: True when string matches IPv6 full or compressed colon-hex form.
validation.is_phone_e164: True when string matches E.164 +[1-9]digits shape (2-15 digits total).

id.random_string: Random alphanumeric string of given length.

encoding.base64_decode: Decode base64 to bytes; fail on invalid input.
encoding.hex_decode: Decode hex to bytes; fail on invalid input.
encoding.url_encode: Percent-encode string for URL query/path components.
encoding.base64url_encode: Encode bytes/string to unpadded URL-safe base64.
encoding.base64url_decode: Decode URL-safe base64 to bytes; fail on invalid input.

path.join_path: Join path segments with single separator, skipping empty parts.
path.normalize_path: Collapse duplicate separators and trailing slash (except root).

object.pick: Return a new object containing only the requested keys.
object.omit: Return a new object excluding the requested keys.
object.get_path: Read a nested value using dot-separated path segments.
object.set_path: Write a nested value using dot-separated path segments, creating objects as needed.
object.has_path: True when dot-separated path exists and value is not null/none.
object.deep_merge: Recursively merge objects; later values override scalars and arrays.
object.shallow_merge: Merge top-level keys; later object wins per key.
object.compact: Remove null/none entries from an object map.
object.keys: Return object keys in insertion order.
object.values: Return object values in insertion order.

crypto.sha256_hash: Compute SHA-256 digest and return lowercase hex.
crypto.hmac_sha256: Compute HMAC-SHA256 and return lowercase hex.
crypto.secure_compare: Constant-time comparison of two strings; false when lengths differ.

optional.coalesce: Return the first non-null, non-blank value.
optional.default_if_blank: Return default when value is null/blank, otherwise trimmed value.

result.ok: Construct a successful result carrying a value.
result.err: Construct a failed result carrying an error message.
result.unwrap_or: Return value on success, default on failure.

i18n.format_number_locale: Format number using BCP47 locale separators.
i18n.format_datetime_locale: Format UTC instant using locale-oriented pattern.
i18n.parse_number_locale: Parse grouped number string for en-US or de-DE separators; null/none on failure.

boolean.parse_bool: Parse common boolean strings (true/false/1/0/yes/no); null on unknown.
boolean.is_truthy: True for non-blank strings other than false/0/no/off (case-insensitive).

compare.deep_equal: Structural equality for JSON-like scalars, arrays, and objects; object key order ignored.
compare.deep_clone: Deep copy JSON-like scalars, arrays, and objects; returned structure is independent.

currency.is_currency_code: True when value is a supported ISO 4217 code in currency metadata.
currency.minor_unit_exponent: Decimal places for standard major-unit amounts; null/none for unknown codes.
currency.to_minor_units: Convert major-unit amount to integer minor units using currency exponent.
currency.from_minor_units: Convert integer minor units to major-unit amount.
currency.format_currency: Format major-unit amount with locale separators and currency symbol.

bloom.create: Create an empty Bloom filter for expected item count and false-positive rate (0.0001-0.25).
bloom.estimate_bit_count: Compute bit array length m from expected items and false-positive rate.
bloom.estimate_hash_count: Compute hash function count k from expected items and bit array length.
bloom.add: Insert a UTF-8 string into the filter.
bloom.might_contain: True when all hash positions are set; false negatives never occur for added items.

bytes.format_bytes: Format a non-negative byte count using 1024-based units and fixed decimal places (default 1) for KB and above.
